response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates exceptional software engineering expertise and polyglot capabilities, this role requires specialized embedded networking and security domain knowledge that is entirely absent from his background. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is a required qualification he does not possess. His 15+ years of experience are concentrated in web, mobile, and high-level application development rather than embedded firmware, Linux kernel work, or network security technologies. The career trajectory and technical stack represent a fundamental mismatch for a mid-senior embedded firmware security engineer role.
strengths: Extensive software development experience (15+ years) demonstrating ability to own complete development lifecycles from design through deployment
Proven polyglot programming skills (D, C, C++, Node.js, TypeScript) showing capacity to learn new languages and technologies quickly
Strong track record building and maintaining CI/CD infrastructure, DevOps systems, and development environments at scale
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency—a critical requirement. Resume lists only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic)
Career entirely focused on web, mobile, and application development with no demonstrable embedded systems, firmware, or low-level Linux kernel experience
Zero evidence of security domain expertise, networking protocols (Layer 2/3), or embedded device technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, switch operation, kernel vulnerabilities)
